name: merge
context: Merge pull requests via PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pull_number}/merge or gh pr merge
info: Merge pull requests using three strategies: merge commit, squash, or rebase. Check merge readiness via the `mergeable` field, ensure required status checks from [[github/actions]] pass, and confirm review approvals from [[github/pulls/review]]. Use `octokit.pulls.merge()` or `gh pr merge`. Branch protection rules in [[github/repos/settings]] control which merge methods are allowed and what prerequisites must be satisfied. See [[github/auth]] for authentication.
body:
# Merge Pull Requests

Merge a pull request after reviews and CI checks pass.

## gh CLI

```bash
# Merge with merge commit (default)
gh pr merge 42

# Squash merge (single commit)
gh pr merge 42 --squash

# Rebase merge (linear history)
gh pr merge 42 --rebase

# Delete branch after merge
gh pr merge 42 --squash --delete-branch

# Auto-merge when checks pass
gh pr merge 42 --auto --squash

# Merge with custom commit message
gh pr merge 42 --squash --subject "feat: add auth (#42)" --body "Adds JWT-based authentication"
```

## REST API

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";
const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// Check if PR is mergeable
const { data: pr } = await octokit.pulls.get({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
});
console.log(pr.mergeable, pr.mergeable_state);
// mergeable_state: clean, dirty, blocked, unstable, unknown

// Merge with squash
await octokit.pulls.merge({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
  merge_method: "squash",
  commit_title: "feat: add user authentication (#42)",
  commit_message: "Adds JWT-based auth with refresh tokens.\n\nCo-authored-by: bot <bot@example.com>",
});

// Delete the branch after merge
await octokit.git.deleteRef({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  ref: "heads/feature/auth",
});

// Enable auto-merge (requires branch protection)
await octokit.pulls.updateBranch({
  owner: "my-org",
  repo: "my-project",
  pull_number: 42,
});
```

```bash
# REST via curl
curl -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GITHUB_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
  https://api.github.com/repos/owner/repo/pulls/42/merge \
  -d '{"merge_method": "squash", "commit_title": "feat: add auth (#42)"}'
```

## Merge Strategies

| Strategy | `merge_method` | Result | Best For |
|----------|---------------|--------|----------|
| Merge commit | `"merge"` | Preserves all commits, adds merge commit | Feature branches with meaningful commit history |
| Squash | `"squash"` | Combines all commits into one | Feature branches with messy commit history |
| Rebase | `"rebase"` | Replays commits on base, linear history | Small, atomic changes |

## Mergeable State

The `mergeable_state` field indicates readiness:

| State | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `clean` | All checks pass, no conflicts, ready to merge |
| `dirty` | Merge conflicts exist — resolve first |
| `blocked` | Branch protection requirements not met |
| `unstable` | Some checks pending or failing |
| `unknown` | GitHub is computing merge status (retry shortly) |

## Auto-Merge

Enable auto-merge to automatically merge when all requirements are met:

```bash
# gh CLI — merge automatically when checks pass
gh pr merge 42 --auto --squash --delete-branch

# Disable auto-merge
gh pr merge 42 --disable-auto
```

Auto-merge requires branch protection to be enabled on the base branch. The PR will merge automatically once all required status checks pass and required reviews are submitted.

## Update Branch

Keep the PR branch up to date with the base branch:

```bash
# Update PR branch with latest base branch changes
gh api repos/owner/repo/pulls/42/update-branch -X PUT
```

sections.title: Merge strategies
sections.body: Three methods: `merge` (preserves commits + merge commit), `squash` (single commit), `rebase` (linear replay). Configure allowed methods per-repo via [[github/repos/settings]]. Squash is most common for feature branches. Rebase preserves individual commits without a merge commit. Merge commit preserves full history including branch topology.

sections.title: Checking merge readiness
sections.body: Check `pr.mergeable` (boolean) and `pr.mergeable_state` (clean/dirty/blocked/unstable/unknown) via `octokit.pulls.get()`. `unknown` means GitHub is still computing — retry after a short delay. `blocked` means branch protection requirements (reviews, checks) are not met. `dirty` means merge conflicts must be resolved.

sections.title: Auto-merge
sections.body: Enable auto-merge with `gh pr merge --auto` or via the GraphQL `enablePullRequestAutoMerge` mutation. The PR merges automatically when all required checks pass and reviews are approved. Requires branch protection on the base branch. Supports specifying the merge method. Disable with `gh pr merge --disable-auto`.

sections.title: Branch cleanup after merge
sections.body: Delete the head branch after merge with `gh pr merge --delete-branch`, `octokit.git.deleteRef()`, or configure the repo to auto-delete branches via [[github/repos/settings]] (`delete_branch_on_merge: true`). Keeping branches clean prevents clutter and confusion.
